summary: Iteration 3 — change-password endpoint, /api/chart/{symbol} (Yahoo), and Resend email integration. Backend pytest: 38/38 PASSED (added 12 new tests: 6 change-password, 5 chart, 1 email side-effect). Frontend Playwright: ⚙ change-password button visible in navbar (data-testid='change-pw-btn'); modal opens; success path produces 'Password updated' toast; logout + relogin with new password succeeds; password restored to admin123. Chart modal opens via open-chart-<SYMBOL> click on Market tab — 1D view renders 77 rects + 93 SVG lines (candles + wicks), 1Y view renders 249 rects with previousClose ReferenceLine visible; Buy button closes chart and opens TradeModal; Close button dismisses chart. Resend email: admin notification to scalesupportteam2@gmail.com confirmed in backend logs (Email sent to=scalesupportteam2@gmail.com id=...); applicant emails to non-verified test addresses log expected WARNING (Resend test-mode 403 — not a code defect).

minor.issue: Resend test mode rejects emails to non-verified recipients with 403; expected. Logged as WARNING. To deliver to applicants in production, verify a domain at resend.com/domains and update SENDER_EMAIL.

ui_bugs.issue: Client-side validation errors (newPassword <6 chars, confirm mismatch, same as current) are not surfaced as visible inline error text — query for [class*='text-red'] / [class*='error'] returned empty after invalid submits, while toast appears only on success. Backend 400/401 responses on submit also do not produce a visible toast/error in the modal. Users see no feedback on bad input.

issues: Recharts ResponsiveContainer emits 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings on initial render (10+ occurrences). Persisted from iter 2 — set explicit minWidth/minHeight on the inner ResponsiveContainer to silence.

action_items: ChangePasswordModal: add visible inline error rendering for client-side validation (length<6, confirm mismatch, same as current) and for backend 400/401 responses (currently only success toast shown).
(Optional) Verify a domain at resend.com to deliver applicant approval/reject emails outside Resend test mode.
(Optional) Silence Recharts width(-1) warnings by giving the chart's ResponsiveContainer an explicit min size.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/chart/{symbol}: Yahoo Finance is hit on every request with no caching — heavy users could trigger Yahoo rate limits. Consider a 30–60s in-memory TTL cache keyed by (symbol, range, interval).
/api/auth/change-password: no rate-limit applied (unlike signup/login/reapply). A malicious actor with a valid token could brute-force currentPassword. Add rate_limit('changepw:<userId>', 10, 60).
server.py is 827 lines — past the 700-line guideline; split into routers/auth.py, routers/admin.py, routers/trade.py, routers/chart.py.

emailer.py reads RESEND_API_KEY/SENDER_EMAIL/ADMIN_EMAIL via os.environ.get on every call (good for hot-reload), but module-level constants SENDER_EMAIL/ADMIN_EMAIL/PUBLIC_APP_URL at top are now dead code — remove for clarity.
chart endpoint: variable name `l` (lowercase L) shadows builtins and triggers ambiguous-naming lint warnings — rename to `lo` or `low`.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on requested flows (ChangePassword UI feedback caveat noted as MEDIUM bug)

test_credentials: admin / admin123 (RESTORED at end of testing — verified by test_admin_password_round_trip_stays_admin123 + frontend roundtrip). demo_user / demo123.
seed_data_creation: 12 ephemeral pending users (uuid prefix) created via /api/auth/signup during test run; approved/rejected/revoked through admin endpoints. No persistent data added.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend ChangePasswordModal data-testids: change-pw-modal (root), cp-current, cp-new, cp-confirm, cp-submit (NOT cpw-*). ChartModal: chart-modal, range-1d|5d|1mo|3mo|6mo|1y, chart-buy, chart-sell, chart-close. Market table: open-chart-<SYMBOL> on each row. ABCAPITAL is the alphabetical first chart-clickable in default sort. Resend test-mode quirk: ONLY emails to scalesupportteam2@gmail.com deliver; all other recipients log WARNING — that's expected, not a bug. To verify email actually fired, grep /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for 'emailer - INFO - Email sent to=scalesupportteam2@gmail.com'.
